briefs.title: BC Business Corporations Regulation updated to recognize Indigenous nation ownership structures
briefs.summary: BC updated its Business Corporations Regulation to formally recognize Indigenous nations as a type of owner in corporate structures. This means a corporation can now be classified as 'wholly owned' by one or more Indigenous nations — including through holding companies (corporations owned by those nations) or intermediaries (third parties acting on their behalf). The update also adds a clear definition for 'professional limited liability partnerships' (professional firms like law or accounting practices that have limited personal liability for their members).
briefs.action_required: If your business involves Indigenous nation ownership or a professional LLP structure, review your ownership records for compliance.
briefs.industries: Professional Services, Finance & Insurance, Natural Resources
